criteria.name: Lead role in distinguished productions
criteria.evidence: Director (lead creative role) on all four feature documentaries; last feature official selection at Sundance, IDFA, and Hot Docs
criteria.rationale: Four features as lead director, with the most recent selected by three internationally recognized major documentary festivals, clearly establishes a lead role in distinguished productions.

criteria.name: National or international recognition
criteria.evidence: Official selections at Sundance (US), IDFA (Netherlands), and Hot Docs (Canada); NYT Critic's Pick
criteria.rationale: Selection at three major international festivals across multiple countries and a prominent U.S. critical designation demonstrate international recognition, though no specific awards won were described.

criteria.name: Reviews & press
criteria.evidence: New York Times Critic's Pick and positive Variety review
criteria.rationale: Coverage in two of the most prominent U.S. film/entertainment publications satisfies the reviews and press criterion.

criteria.name: Record of major commercial or critical success
criteria.evidence: Theatrical and streaming distribution deal secured; festival selections at Sundance, IDFA, Hot Docs; NYT Critic's Pick
criteria.rationale: A distribution deal plus top-tier festival placements and critical recognition indicate notable success, though specific commercial metrics (box office, streaming numbers) or awards were not described.

criteria.name: Recognition from organizations & experts
criteria.evidence: Voting membership in the Directors Guild of America and International Documentary Association
criteria.rationale: DGA voting membership reflects peer recognition within a distinguished professional organization, but no described expert testimonials, jury prizes, or formal honors from organizations were mentioned to fully satisfy this criterion.

criteria.name: High salary or remuneration
criteria.evidence: No compensation figures, contract values, or comparator salary data described
criteria.rationale: No evidence of remuneration was provided in the described background.

gaps: Provide documented salary or fee data demonstrating remuneration substantially above peers in documentary direction
Obtain expert opinion letters from recognized figures in documentary film attesting to extraordinary achievement
Document any jury prizes, nominations, or formal awards won at Sundance, IDFA, Hot Docs, or other festivals
Supply commercial performance metrics for the distribution deal (e.g., theatrical grosses, streaming reach) to strengthen commercial success criterion
Gather additional press coverage beyond the two outlets described, especially international reviews, to reinforce national/international recognition
disclaimer: This is general informational guidance only, not legal advice. Immigration law is fact-specific and changes frequently. An attorney of record licensed to practice law is required to review your petition and advise on your situation before anything is filed with USCIS.
